dsl_rule.translation_notes: Entry conditions detect spike reversal pattern: (1) Price approaches/exceeds recent high (resistance), (2) Above-average volatility via ATR vs historical vol, (3) Price rejection below the spike high. Exit on breach above spike high indicating failed resistance. The spike itself (local top with above-average vol bracketed by lower days) is implied by recent price structure. Using dc_upper as rolling resistance proxy for spike high over ~5-7 day window.

backtest_rule.reason: Entry conditions detect spike reversal pattern: (1) Price approaches/exceeds recent high (resistance), (2) Above-average volatility via ATR vs historical vol, (3) Price rejection below the spike high. Exit on breach above spike high indicating failed resistance. The spike itself (local top with above-average vol bracketed by lower days) is implied by recent price structure. Using dc_upper as rolling resistance proxy for spike high over ~5-7 day window.

source_rule_text: When prices rise again after an upward spike (a local top marked by above-average volatility bracketed by two lower days), sell at resistance formed by the high price of the spike.
